title: GPU image supply-chain signing and provenance playbook
preview:
Summary:
This playbook defines the standard process for producing, signing, annotating, and promoting GPU runtime images and model-serving artifacts through our CI/CD pipelines. It combines practical pipeline snippets (Buildkite + GitHub Actions), verification procedures (Cosign + Rekor), SBOM handling, Terraform attachment patterns, and runbook steps for suspected supply-chain compromises. The target audience is infra engineers, platform SRE, and security engineers responsible for image supply-chain integrity.

Goals:
- Ensure all GPU images used in production are signed and traceable to a reproducible build.
- Produce SBOMs and provenance records that satisfy SLSA level 2+ checks for CI attestation.
- Provide automated gating for image promotion with built-in verification steps.
- Define rapid incident procedures when provenance/signature checks fail.

Scope and assumptions:
- Applies to: container images (GPU runtime images, model runtime bundles, driver shim images), OCI artifacts stored in our internal registry (registry.redwood-infra.internal).
- Does not cover OS-level firmware updates or third-party marketplace images unless imported via our import workflow.
- Requires: access to KMS for signing keys, Rekor transparency log, Cosign/Notation installed in CI runners, and the image-promotion Terraform module (see section).

Definitions:
- Artifact: OCI image or model artifact that will be deployed to serving clusters.
- Provenance: machine-readable statement (in-toto/Cyber/Provenance v0.1) that records build inputs, outputs, and the CI job that created it.
- SBOM: Software Bill of Materials produced by syft (or equivalent) describing packages in the image.

High-level flow:
1. Build stage (ephemeral runner)
   - Reproducible build using pinned base images and deterministic build args.
   - Produce image digest, SBOM (syft --format json), and provenance file (in-toto or cosign-attestation).
2. Sign stage
   - Cosign signs the image digest using KMS-backed keys (See "Key management").
   - Attestations (SLSA predicate) are uploaded to Rekor and associated with the digest.
3. Verification stage (pre-promotion)
   - Admission checks and promotion pipelines verify signature + sbom + predicate.
4. Promotion stage
   - Terraform module attaches the immutable digest to the appropriate image alias and updates the Promotion ledger (Terraform state + tagging).

Key management:
- Production signing keys are stored in a customer-managed KMS project: kms://projects/redwood-prod/locations/global/keyRings/supply-chain/cryptoKeys/gpu-signing-key
- CI uses short-lived signers: workflows request an ephemeral signing token via the sign-service (internal), which calls KMS.sign.
- Rotate keys annually; emergency rotation steps are in the "Emergency key rotation" appendix.

Tooling and versions (recommended):
- cosign v2.7+ for signing and attestations
- rekor-server v0.12+ (hosted at rekor.redwood-infra.internal)
- syft v0.72+ to generate SBOMs
- in-toto or SLSA predicate generator (bundled in our buildkit image)

CI pipeline snippets
- Buildkite (reference):

  steps:
    - label: "build: gpu-runtime-image"
      command: |
        set -euo pipefail
        export IMAGE_NAME=registry.redwood-infra.internal/gpu-runtime:${BUILDKITE_COMMIT} 
        buildctl build --frontend=v0.10 --opt source=.,platform=linux/amd64,linux/ppc64le -o type=image,name=$IMAGE_NAME,push=true 
        syft $IMAGE_NAME -o json > sbom-$BUILDKITE_COMMIT.json 
        cosign attest --key kms://projects/redwood-prod/... $IMAGE_NAME --type slsa \
          --predicate-file provenance-$BUILDKITE_COMMIT.json
        cosign sign --key kms://projects/redwood-prod/... $IMAGE_NAME

- GitHub Actions (promotion job):

  name: verify-and-promote
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Verify signature
      run: |
        IMAGE=registry.redwood-infra.internal/gpu-runtime:${{ inputs.digest }}
        cosign verify --key kms://projects/redwood-prod/... $IMAGE
        cosign verify-attestation --type slsa --key kms://projects/redwood-prod/... $IMAGE
    - name: Check SBOM policy
      run: |
        jq '.artifacts | length' sbom-${{ inputs.digest }}.json > /dev/null || exit 1
    - name: Terraform promote (uses image-promotion module)
      run: |
        cd infra/terraform/modules/image-promotion && terraform init && terraform apply -var image_digest=${{ inputs.digest }} -auto-approve

Policy table (minimum gate checks before promotion):
| Gate | Criteria | Failure action | Owner |
|-----|----------|---------------|-------|
| signature present | cosign sign found for digest | block promotion | infra-cicd |
| attestation SLSA | predicate includes build id, builder id, input digests | block promotion | security |
| SBOM completeness | >= 90% package coverage, no high severity unknown packages | request manual review | platform-security |
| base image policy | base image digest must be in approved list | block promotion | infra-inventory |

Terraform module usage
- Module: modules/image-promotion (inputs and example table)
| Input name | Type | Example | Notes |
| image_digest | string | sha256:abcd... | mandatory; immutable pointer used by runtime config |
| repo_alias | string | gpu-runtime:stable | Optional alias that will be updated to point to digest |
| promoted_by | string | ci/buildkite#1234 | audit field |

Verification in cluster (runtime gating)
- Kubernetes admission controller (image-integrity-advisor) enforces:
  - If image tag is not pinned to a digest, deny deploys in production namespaces.
  - Verify cosign signature via Rekor and validate predicate fields: build_id matches allowed builders; SBOM attached URL reachable.
- Example policy snippet (Gatekeeper-style YAML):
  apiVersion: constraints.gatekeeper.sh/v1beta1
  kind: K8sAllowedImages
  metadata:
    name: require-signed-images
  spec:
    enforcementAction: deny
    parameters:
      signatureVerification: true

Runbook: signature/attestation failure during promotion
1. Alert fires: alert name = CI.ImagePromotion.SignatureMissing (pager to infra-cicd).
2. Initial triage (first 15 minutes):
   - Confirm digest in registry: 
     curl -s https://registry.redwood-infra.internal/v2/_catalog | jq .
   - Re-run cosign verify locally from a secure bastion: cosign verify --key <kms-ref> image@sha256:...
3. If verify fails due to missing attestation:
   - Check Rekor: curl -s https://rekor.redwood-infra.internal/api/v1/log/entries?hash=<hash>
   - If Rekor has entry but CI didn't upload, escalate to platform-ci team and re-run attestation upload job.
4. If cosign verify fails due to key error:
   - Check sign-service logs: /var/log/sign-service/*.log on signers.redwood-infra.internal
   - Validate KMS permission: gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy ... (or aws kms get-key-policy ...)
5. If signing key compromised (see Indicators of Compromise below):
   - Trigger "Emergency key rotation" (section below).
   - Revoke affected promotions and schedule rebuilds for all images signed with the key.

Indicators of compromise:
- Multiple unexpected signatures using same key from different CI jobs outside scheduled windows.
- Rekor entries with mismatched build ids or builder identifiers.
- Missing or tampered SBOMs in the registry for promoted digests.

Emergency key rotation (high level):
1. Mark current key as compromised in the key registry and revoke signing permissions.
2. Provision new KMS key: kms://projects/redwood-prod/.../gpu-signing-key-v2.
3. Update sign-service to use new key; block all promotions until the rollout is complete.
4. Rebuild all artifacts that used the old key and push signed alternatives.
5. Update Terraform image-promotion ledger to point to newly signed digests.
6. Post-incident: rotate all CI runner tokens and audit who used the old key.

Monitoring & Alerts:
- Alert: CI.ImagePromotion.SignatureMissing (severity: warning) - triggered on failed signature/attestation check during promotion.
- Alert: Registry.SBOM.Missing (severity: critical) - triggered when promoted digest lacks SBOM.
- Telemetry: Track median time between build completion and successful Rekor entry (goal <= 2 minutes).

Ownership and contacts:
- Image signing pipeline owner: infra-cicd@redwood.com (primary: Ethan Park)
- Key material owner: security-kms@redwood.com (primary: Marta Svensson)
- Incident lead for rotation: oncall-security (bookable via PagerDuty)

Appendices:
A. Example cosign sign command (KMS):
   cosign sign --key kms://projects/redwood-prod/locations/global/keyRings/supply-chain/cryptoKeys/gpu-signing-key \
     registry.redwood-infra.internal/gpu-runtime@sha256:abcd1234

B. Example Rekor query (to validate attestation):
   curl -s 'https://rekor.redwood-infra.internal/api/v1/log/entries?hash=sha256:abcd1234' | jq .

C. Rebuild checklist (for rotated keys):
- Update CI templates to point at new key reference.
- Regenerate SBOMs and provenance; verify locally before mass promotion.
- Run promotion in small canary clusters (canary x 5 nodes) for 24h before global rollout.

D. Related policies and docs:
- Secure SDLC: /confluence/security-and-compliance/secure-sdlc
- Image promotion module: /confluence/eng-infra/terraform-and-iac/image-promotion-module-spec

Revision history:
- 2023-06-15: created (Priya Malhotra)
- 2024-08-02: added Rekor and SBOM policy table (Marta Svensson)
- 2025-02-10: emergency key rotation appendix and Terraform input table (Ethan Park)
